rows.display_name: BBB permeability increase -> peripheral immune infiltration
rows.support_reason: Direct directional support is present in 2 causal edge-backed paper(s) and reinforced by 3 claim-backed paper(s).
rows.statement_text: Current TBI evidence supports a directional transition in which BBB permeability increase facilitates peripheral immune infiltration.
rows.causal_direction_notes: Upstream lane blood_brain_barrier_failure is currently treated as the initiating side of this transition. || Downstream lane neuroinflammation_microglial_state_change is currently treated as the receiving side of this transition.
rows.evidence_gaps: Some supporting rows are abstract-only and should be weighted cautiously. || Downstream lane is still seeded/provisional, so this transition should remain bounded.
rows.example_signals: edge: PMID 41709060 BBB disruption drives peripheral immune cell infiltration || edge: PMID 41740873 BBB disruption drives Peripheral macrophage infiltration || claim: PMID 41683989 TBI-induced BBB breakdown promotes neuroinflammation. || claim: PMID 41709060 Increased BBB permeability allows peripheral leukocyte infiltration.

rows.support_reason: Direct directional support is present in 2 causal edge-backed paper(s) and reinforced by 2 claim-backed paper(s).
rows.statement_text: Current TBI evidence supports a transition in which glymphatic failure contributes to tau or broader pathogenic protein accumulation.
rows.causal_direction_notes: Upstream lane glymphatic_astroglial_clearance_failure is currently treated as the initiating side of this transition. || Downstream lane tau_proteinopathy_progression is currently treated as the receiving side of this transition.
rows.evidence_gaps: Some supporting rows are abstract-only and should be weighted cautiously. || Downstream lane is still seeded/provisional, so this transition should remain bounded. || Upstream lane still needs stronger longitudinal support.
rows.example_signals: edge: PMID 41179995 Glymphatic dysfunction increases Accumulation of metabolic waste (e.g., tau, S100β) || edge: PMID 41700070 Glymphatic dysfunction increases Hyperphosphorylated tau accumulation || claim: PMID 41700070 Glymphatic dysfunction leads to pathogenic protein accumulation. || claim: PMID 41747594 NAMPT-EVs improve glymphatic clearance of acetylated tau.

rows.support_reason: Transition is presently carried more by directional claims or associative edges than by multiple direct causal edges, so it remains provisional.
rows.statement_text: Current TBI evidence suggests that axonal degeneration and white-matter injury contribute to chronic network dysfunction and impaired recovery trajectories.
rows.causal_direction_notes: Upstream lane axonal_degeneration is currently treated as the initiating side of this transition. || Downstream lane axonal_degeneration is currently treated as the receiving side of this transition.
rows.evidence_gaps: Transition still needs denser direct support before it should be treated as hardened. || Some supporting rows are abstract-only and should be weighted cautiously. || Downstream lane is still seeded/provisional, so this transition should remain bounded. || Upstream lane still needs stronger longitudinal support.
rows.example_signals: edge: PMID 41192919 White matter FA (limbic-prefrontal) predicts Internalizing/Externalizing behavioral problems || edge: PMID 41577806 axonal_transport_disruption correlates_with cognitive_deficits || edge: PMID 41604614 diffuse_axonal_injury predicts unfavorable_recovery_trajectory || edge: PMID 41740080 Limbic White Matter Degradation correlates_with Cognitive Decline

rows.support_reason: Transition has at least one direct edge-backed anchor, but the evidence is not yet dense enough to harden it.
rows.statement_text: Current TBI evidence suggests that mitochondrial ROS or related mitochondrial stress signals amplify inflammasome activation.
rows.causal_direction_notes: Upstream lane mitochondrial_bioenergetic_collapse is currently treated as the initiating side of this transition. || Downstream lane neuroinflammation_microglial_state_change is currently treated as the receiving side of this transition.
rows.evidence_gaps: Transition still needs denser direct support before it should be treated as hardened. || Timing support is incomplete or only partially aligned with the expected transition window. || Downstream lane is still seeded/provisional, so this transition should remain bounded. || Upstream lane still needs stronger longitudinal support.
rows.example_signals: edge: PMID 41480492 mtROS increases NLRP3_inflammasome || claim: PMID 41103638 mtDAMPs drive neuroinflammation and cell death via TLR9, NLRP3, and cGAS-STING pathways. || claim: PMID 41480492 MAM-derived stress signals promote NLRP3 inflammasome assembly. || claim: PMID 41709060 NLRP3 inflammasome activation and mitochondrial dysfunction amplify neuroinflammatory signaling.
